checks.a_analysis: The output content closely follows the source text. All headings and body text elements have a direct basis in the source document. The subsection title 'Contact Information' is an organizational addition that categorizes existing contact details, consistent with allowed accessibility enhancements like section labels.

All contact information was verified against page 2 of the source document. The name (Bob Coats), email address (Bob.Coats@osbm.nc.gov), and phone number ((919) 807-4781) are identical to the original text.

The output successfully converts both pages of the source PDF. Page 1's main text, sidebars (Representation, Funding, Planning), and infographic are present. Page 2's timeline (converted to a table), program descriptions (BAS, LUCA, PSAP, CCC, GSS/SDRP), and contact information are all included.

The timeline graphic from page 2 of the source is appropriately converted into a table structure. The table contains a header row for the years 2014-2021 and a data row containing the corresponding activity descriptions. No rows or information from the timeline are missing.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: Each paragraph and bulleted section was cross-referenced. Detailed information such as specific dates in the LUCA and PSAP sections, and monetary figures in the Funding section, are fully preserved without omission.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: Textual content is faithful to the source. Minor formatting adjustments (such as line breaks within the 'Funding' sidebar text) do not alter the meaning or data. Punctuation and numbering (e.g., $1,623, P.L.94-171) are accurate.

checks.a_analysis: I have reviewed the entire JSON output and compared it with the source PDF. No paragraphs, headings, or structural blocks are duplicated in the output. Text such as '2020 Census' appears multiple times, but only as it does in the source document.

The heading hierarchy is logical and follows a consistent structure. The main document title is defined as an H2. Major content areas are organized as subsections (which render as H3). Section 2 also uses a title that functions as an H2, with its 'Contact Information' subsection nested as an H3. There are no skips in heading levels.

The conversion correctly identifies the timeline graphic on Page 2 as a table. The structure (8 columns for years 2014-2021) matches the logical flow of the source. Row 1 contains the headers (years), and Row 2 contains the corresponding descriptive text blocks. Column spans and data mapping are accurate to the source timeline.

The reading order faithfully represents the source document. On Page 1, it proceeds from the title/map to the main body text, then through the three sidebar blocks ('Representation', 'Funding', 'Planning'), and concludes with the 2020 Census infographic at the bottom. Page 2 follows a top-to-bottom flow (Timeline -> Program details -> Funnel graphic -> Contact info). This matches the logical and visual intent of the source.

The document contains several sections that could be interpreted as lists (e.g., the program descriptions on Page 2). The source PDF does not use formal bullet points or numbering for these items, instead using bold labels followed by text. The JSON preserves this structure by using bold text within paragraph elements. No list items are merged or incorrectly nested.

checks.check_name: Heading count mismatch between JSON and HTML
checks.a_analysis: The HTML metadata reports 8 headings in HTML and 1 in the semantic.json headings count. This discrepancy is due to the audit tool counting only explicit elements of 'type: heading' in the JSON (of which there is 1), while the renderer correctly promotes 'section.title' and 'subsection.title' properties into HTML headings. Counting the 'type: heading' element, the 1 section title, and the 5 subsection titles (Census Day, Representation, Funding, Planning, Contact Information) results in 7 logical headings. The 8th heading in HTML is likely a generated page title or table header. The structure is preserved correctly.

The JSON contains one explicit email link for 'Bob.Coats@osbm.nc.gov'. The HTML metadata reports 2 links. This difference is expected, as renderers commonly auto-link phone numbers (e.g., (919) 807-4781) found in the text for mobile accessibility. The primary contact links are present and accurate to the source.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output successfully adapts the PDF's layout to a single-column responsive format. The sidebar content from page 1 ('Representation', 'Funding', 'Planning') is correctly placed following the main introductory text. The timeline graphic from page 2 is converted into a structured table, which maintains the chronological organization of information. The overall flow of information matches the source's intent.

The timeline graphic from page 2 of the PDF is rendered as a clean, legible table in the HTML. The years (2014-2021) serve as headers and the corresponding activities are placed clearly in the body cells. Borders and alignment allow for easy reading of the data.

A significant infographic from page 2 of the source PDF is missing in the HTML output. The PDF features a colorful funnel diagram that illustrates how various programs (LUCA, SDRP, PSAP, BAS, GSS) lead to 'Correct addresses', 'Correct boundaries', and 'Correct streets'. This graphic conveys the outcome of the census preparation work. While the program names appear in the text, the specific outcome text ('Correct addresses', etc.) and the visual representation of the process are completely absent from the HTML.
checks.b_evidence_source: Infographic on page 2 showing a funnel with labels LUCA, SDRP, PSAP, BAS, GSS leading to boxes labeled 'Correct addresses', 'Correct boundaries', and 'Correct streets'.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML content transitions directly from the 'School District Review Program (SDRP)' text to the 'Contact Information' header, omitting the funnel graphic and its associated text labels.

checks.a_analysis: No visual content duplication was observed. Each section of text and each graphic (that was included) appears only once in the sequence corresponding to the source document.
Apart from the missing image, the visual quality is high. Text spacing, headings, and the representation of the timeline as a table are all handled well. The document is professional and readable.
